summary: Phase 2 audit batch tested. Backend: 19/19 pytest tests pass — username system + signup auto-gen + /auth/me + /users/search (prefix match, friendship field, capped @20), PATCH /users/username (422/200/409), phone-hash registration + /contacts/match (phone & email matched_via), friend request via @username, block/unblock direction + 403 for non-blocker unblock + edge removal on unblock, email back-compat, image messages (200 with image_base64, 400 empty, 413 oversize >6MB decoded, GET history). Startup log lines 'indexes ensured' and 'legacy usernames backfilled' present. Frontend: All 6 bottom tabs render correctly; Wishlist standalone tab renders wishlist-screen + wishlist-add-button (size-pill absent because test user has no measurements — expected); onboarding wheel pickers (onboard-height/weight) render as scroll wheels; @username friend search shows 'User not found' inline for bogus, PENDING SENT for valid user; chat-camera-button + chat-gallery-button present at /social/chat/{friendId}. CRITICAL FRONTEND BUG: Profile tab crashes with ReferenceError: 'showAdd is not defined' at /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/profile.tsx:172 — orphan Wishlist Add modal references state vars that were deleted when Wishlist became its own tab. This blocks the entire Profile screen.

ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError: showAdd is not defined at app/(tabs)/profile.tsx:172. Orphan <Modal visible={showAdd}> + bound TextInputs (wName/wDesc/wImage/etc.) and a setShowAdd onRequestClose still reference removed Wishlist-Add state. The whole Profile screen renders an Uncaught Error overlay so AvatarDashboard, Brands, Account subtabs are all blocked.

Header shows 'Chat' instead of the friend's name and empty placeholder 'Start the conversation with .' — friend name not loaded into the header context when route is opened directly. Reference: app/social/chat/[friendId].tsx
Label reads 'ADD FRIEND BY EMAIL' and placeholder is 'friend@example.com', but the field accepts @username too. Misleading copy.

Messages tab shows 'No messages yet' even when friend list has accepted friends; no entry point to start a chat from Messages until at least one message exists. Friends sub-tab also does not appear to expose a 'Chat' action on each friend row (could not be confirmed because of empty state during test).

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): Delete the orphan Wishlist Add Modal block in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/profile.tsx around line 171-end (Modal visible={showAdd} ... and its closing </Modal>). It still references showAdd/setShowAdd, wName/setWName, wDesc/setWDesc, wImage/setWImage, addWish, etc. which were removed when Wishlist was moved to /(tabs)/wishlist.tsx. Without this fix Profile tab is unusable.
Rename the Social add-friend label/placeholder to 'ADD FRIEND BY @USERNAME OR EMAIL' (or similar) so users discover the new @username flow.
Show friend name in chat header (load friend doc via /api/friends or pass name in route params) and fix empty placeholder 'Start the conversation with .'
Allow opening a chat from the Friends sub-tab (or surface accepted friends in Messages tab with empty thread state) so users can start chatting with a brand-new friend.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/profile.tsx still imports Modal/KeyboardAvoidingView and renders the dead Wishlist-Add modal (lines ~171-230). Dead/leftover code crashes the screen.

Backend /api/messages oversize check is on decoded bytes (raw_b64 length * 3 / 4 > 6 MB). Spec says '~7 MB raw base64' would 413 — 7 MB raw decodes to ~5.25 MB so it does NOT 413. This is consistent with the spec text intent (≤6 MB decoded), but the spec example value is misleading. No code change required; just keep this in mind for QA payload sizes.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (6 of 7 frontend checks pass; Profile tab crashes; chat header copy minor)

seed_data_creation: Created QA users via signup with timestamped emails qa+ph2_userA_<ts>@closetai.com and qa+ph2_userB_<ts>@closetai.com for backend tests. Also reused alice@closetai.com and accepted a friend-request between test@closetai.com and alice@closetai.com to enable the chat-camera UI check.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Once main agent removes the orphan Wishlist Add modal from profile.tsx, retest: (a) Profile tab renders AvatarDashboard + Brands + Account subtabs without crash; (b) Account subtab does NOT have a 'Wishlist' option; (c) Avatar completion bar still ticks up. The backend phase-2 suite at /app/backend/tests/test_phase2_audit.py is the regression target — keep it green.
rca_of_issue: Profile crash RCA: In moving Wishlist out of the Profile tab into a standalone (tabs)/wishlist.tsx route, the state hooks (showAdd, wName, wDesc, wImage, etc.) and the addWish/loadWishlist callbacks were removed from Profile, but the JSX still contains the <Modal visible={showAdd} ...> + its internal TextInputs and the setShowAdd onRequestClose handler. Because the component references undefined identifiers at render time, React throws a ReferenceError that the dev-mode RedBox surfaces as 'Uncaught Error: showAdd is not defined' at profile.tsx:27:27 (the line reported is the first hook line; the actual offending reference is line 172). Fix: delete lines 171-end-of-modal (around line ~230) plus any stale imports (Modal, KeyboardAvoidingView, Platform if unused). Re-run the frontend test.
